dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}! Please, stop! My child is gone and every second you stand here is a second they're getting further away!
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They took her last night. Right out of the house while we slept. {target_npc} heard the door close and ran out, but all they saw were {enemy_type} shapes disappearing into the treeline {location_hint}. The tracks are clear: heavy boots and dragging marks. She's alive, {player_name}, I know it. They wouldn't go to the trouble of a quiet abduction just to... I can't finish that thought. The guards are organizing a search, but they're too slow, too cautious. My daughter doesn't have time for caution.
dialogueChunks.outro: She's ten years old and she's out there in the dark with monsters. Please. I will give you everything I own.

playerResponses.text: Keep your possessions. Just show me the tracks and I'll bring her home.
I'll follow the trail now. Don't lose hope.
I'm deeply sorry, but tracking at night through hostile territory is beyond me.

dialogueChunks.intro: Something is taking people from {location}, {player_name}. Not killing them: taking them. And nobody knows where they go.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three people have vanished in the last two weeks. First the miller's son, then a young woman from the orchards, then {target_npc}'s apprentice. No bodies, no blood, no signs of struggle: just empty beds in the morning and families torn apart. A hunter found strange tracks {location_hint} that don't match any animal she knows. {enemy_type} tracks, but organized: lines, formations, deliberate patterns. Someone or something is selecting people and spiriting them away.
dialogueChunks.outro: The settlement is terrified. Doors are barred every night and nobody walks alone anymore. We need to find where they're being taken before the next bed turns up empty.

playerResponses.text: Organized abductions? This isn't random. I'll follow the tracks and find the pattern.
Three people vanished without a trace? That's unsettling. I'll investigate.
Whatever is doing this is methodical and dangerous. I'd rather not be next.

dialogueChunks.intro: I wasn't going to involve outsiders, {player_name}. But we've run out of options and we've run out of time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} was taken in broad daylight. Right from the market square in {location}, in front of a dozen witnesses. A group of {enemy_type} stormed in, grabbed them, and were gone before anyone could draw a weapon. It happened so fast that people are still trying to process what they saw. They headed {location_hint}, moving with a speed that suggests they knew exactly where they were going. The guards gave chase but lost the trail at the river crossing.
dialogueChunks.outro: Broad daylight, {player_name}. They're not even afraid of us anymore. If we don't respond to this, they'll come back for more.

playerResponses.text: Taken in daylight? They're getting bold. I'll pick up the trail at the river.
A coordinated snatch in a crowded market? These aren't ordinary raiders.
If they can grab someone from a crowded square, nowhere is safe. I'm leaving.

dialogueChunks.intro: They left a ransom demand, {player_name}. Nailed to the gate of {location} in blood.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} are holding {target_npc} and four others hostage in their stronghold {location_hint}. The note demands a cartload of {quest_item} delivered within five days, or they'll start sending back fingers. The settlement doesn't have what they're asking for: we'd have to strip every store bare and we still couldn't meet the demand. We need someone to either rescue the hostages directly or gather enough {quest_item} to negotiate, preferably both.
dialogueChunks.outro: Five days, {player_name}. Five days before they start hurting people we love.

playerResponses.text: I'll get the hostages out before the deadline. No ransom needed.
Five days is tight but workable. I'll gather what I can and plan a rescue.
Ransom demands backed by mutilation threats? This is too dangerous.
